document.source_title: WHO certifies the Bahamas for eliminating mother-to-child transmission of HIV

claims.claim_text: The Bahamas has eliminated mother-to-child transmission of HIV.
claims.evidence_text: the World Health Organization (WHO) congratulates The Bahamas for becoming the latest Caribbean nation to be certified as having eliminated the mother-to-child transmission of HIV.

entities.name: World Health Organization
entities.description: The specialized agency of the United Nations responsible for international public health.
entities.evidence_text: the World Health Organization (WHO) congratulates The Bahamas

entities.name: Pan American Health Organization
entities.description: Regional office of the World Health Organization for the Americas.
entities.evidence_text: said Dr Jarbas Barbosa, Director of the Pan American Health Organization (PAHO)

entities.name: UNAIDS
entities.description: Joint United Nations Programme on HIV/AIDS.
entities.evidence_text: said Winnie Byanyima, UNAIDS Executive Director.

entities.name: EMTCT Plus Initiative
entities.description: Aims to eliminate mother-to-child transmission of HIV, syphilis, hepatitis B, and congenital Chagas disease.
entities.evidence_text: The Bahamas’ success is part of the broader EMTCT Plus Initiative

events.event: WHO certifies The Bahamas for eliminating mother-to-child transmission of HIV
events.evidence_text: the World Health Organization (WHO) congratulates The Bahamas for becoming the latest Caribbean nation to be certified

metrics.name: mother-to-child transmission rate of HIV
metrics.evidence_text: reducing the mother-to-child transmission rate of HIV to less than 2%

metrics.name: new pediatric HIV infections
metrics.evidence_text: achieving fewer than 5 new pediatric HIV infections per 1000 live births

metrics.name: antenatal care coverage
metrics.evidence_text: maintaining 95% or higher coverage for antenatal care, HIV testing, and treatment for pregnant women

citations.title: WHO certifies the Bahamas for eliminating mother-to-child transmission of HIV

source_credibility.reason: The information is provided by the World Health Organization, a recognized authority in public health.

geolocation.name: The Bahamas
geolocation.evidence_text: The Bahamas has eliminated mother-to-child transmission of HIV.
